---
title: "Day 1"
---

Day 1 is an afternoon session (Wednesday, July 22, 1:30 to 5:00 pm) in Randall Rollins Building 205 (RRR 205). We open with the forecasting and human-AI teaming lectures, then move into the lab: getting set up in GitHub and VS Code, and completing the first activity, data cleaning.

Each deck below has a download link and a collapsible in-page preview.

## Schedule

+----------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Time           | Session                                                          |
+:===============+:=================================================================+
| 1:30 - 3:00 pm | Introductions                                                    |
|                |                                                                  |
|                | **Lecture 1:** Overview of Forecasting                           |
|                |                                                                  |
|                | **Lecture 2:** Introduction to AI and Human-AI Teaming           |
+----------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 3:00 - 3:30 pm | *Break*                                                          |
+----------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+
| 3:30 - 5:00 pm | **Lecture 3 (Lab):** Introduction to GitHub, VS Code, and Set Up |
|                |                                                                  |
|                | **Activity 1:** Data Cleaning                                    |
+----------------+------------------------------------------------------------------+

## Lecture 1: Overview of Forecasting

Forthcoming.

## Lecture 2: Introduction to AI and Human-AI Teaming

Forthcoming.

## Lab Overview

Over the three days, you will work as a human-AI team to build a national-level influenza hospitalization forecasting pipeline using real CDC data. You define the vision and the rules; the AI helps build and implement the solution. Throughout, you remain the expert making the decisions.

The work is organized around two files:

- **rules.md** (written by you): human-readable specifications that describe *what* the pipeline should do. You edit this to improve results.
- **agents.md** (generated by AI from your rules): agent instructions that describe *how* to execute the pipeline. It is the intermediary between your rules and the code.

For each phase of the lab, you repeat the same human-AI iteration loop:

1.  Edit the rules for that phase.
2.  Generate `agents.md` from the rules document.
3.  Generate the R script from `agents.md`.
4.  Run the script in VS Code and evaluate the output.
5.  Refine the rules, then regenerate `agents.md` and the script.
6.  Push your results to GitHub.

### Lab objectives and end products

| Objective | End products |
|----|----|
| Use AI as a collaborative tool to build, refine, and execute an infectious disease forecasting pipeline | `rules.md` and `agents.md` |
| Translate epidemiological knowledge into structured instructions that guide AI-generated code | `rules.md` and `agents.md` |
| Develop a model to forecast incident influenza hospitalizations at the national level using CDC NHSN data | Forecasting scripts and forecast outputs |
| Evaluate forecast performance and iteratively improve model quality through human-AI teaming | Scripts, outputs, and refined rules |

[Download slides (PPTX)](slides/day1/Lab_Overview.pptx){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Preview: Lab Overview

<iframe src="https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/embed.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fdma-prime.github.io%2FSISMID-2026-Human-AI-Teaming-Approach-to-ID-Modeling%2Fslides%2Fday1%2FLab_Overview.pptx" width="100%" height="600" frameborder="0">

</iframe>
:::

## Lecture 3 (Lab): Getting Set Up

The setup lab has three parts. Together they get your own copy of the course repository onto GitHub, cloned into VS Code with GitHub Copilot enabled, and ready to submit results back to the class repository.

### Introduction to GitHub

Fork the course repository to create your own personal copy under your GitHub account, then open it so it is ready to work in during the lab.

**Goal output:** your own fork at `github.com/your-username/course-repo`, a personal copy of the class repository that you fully control.

[Download slides (PPTX)](slides/day1/Intro_to_GitHub.pptx){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Preview: Introduction to GitHub

<iframe src="https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/embed.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fdma-prime.github.io%2FSISMID-2026-Human-AI-Teaming-Approach-to-ID-Modeling%2Fslides%2Fday1%2FIntro_to_GitHub.pptx" width="100%" height="600" frameborder="0">

</iframe>
:::

### Introduction to VS Code

Install the R extension, turn on GitHub Copilot, connect GitHub and VS Code by cloning your fork, and set up your named working directory inside the USE-ME folder.

**Goal output:** your forked repository cloned into VS Code, plus your own named working directory set as the active folder so everything you create is saved in the right place.

[Download slides (PPTX)](slides/day1/Introduction_to_VS_Code.pptx){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Preview: Introduction to VS Code

<iframe src="https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/embed.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fdma-prime.github.io%2FSISMID-2026-Human-AI-Teaming-Approach-to-ID-Modeling%2Fslides%2Fday1%2FIntroduction_to_VS_Code.pptx" width="100%" height="600" frameborder="0">

</iframe>
:::

### GitHub Commits and Pull Requests

Upload your output files to your fork (via VS Code or the GitHub web portal), then open a pull request to merge those changes into the main class repository. After each phase, commit your changes and submit a pull request so everyone stays in sync.

**Goal output:** an updated GitHub fork with your results committed, and a pull request merging those changes into the class repository.

[Download slides (PPTX)](slides/day1/Commits_PullRequests_Git.pptx){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Preview: GitHub Commits and Pull Requests

<iframe src="https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/embed.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fdma-prime.github.io%2FSISMID-2026-Human-AI-Teaming-Approach-to-ID-Modeling%2Fslides%2Fday1%2FCommits_PullRequests_Git.pptx" width="100%" height="600" frameborder="0">

</iframe>
:::

## Activity 1: Data Cleaning and Visuals

Guided by your data-cleaning rules, an AI agent turns the raw Weekly HRD Metrics file into a tidy dataset and the epidemic curve that summarizes it.

**Goal output:**

| Output | Description |
|----|----|
| `cleaned_flu_admissions.csv` | A tidy dataset with three columns (week, location, value) produced by the cleaning script. |
| Epicurve | An epidemic curve plotting the cleaned weekly admissions. |

**The data-cleaning rules** (already provided in `rules.md`):

1.  Load the Weekly Hospital Respiratory Data (HRD) Metrics by Jurisdiction file from the `data/` folder using readr.
2.  Filter to US only: keep rows where `Geographic aggregation` is "USA".
3.  Select the target column: keep `Total Influenza Admissions`, and drop the others (including RSV and COVID-19).
4.  Reshape to three columns: week, location ("US"), and value.
5.  Format dates: convert the week-ending date to an R Date object and sort ascending.
6.  Save output: write `cleaned_flu_admissions.csv` and the epicurve to the appropriate `output/` subfolder.

**Your tasks:**

1.  Confirm your data: check that the project folder contains the Weekly HRD Metrics CSV.
2.  Review the rules: open `rules.md` and confirm the cleaning rules match those above.
3.  Create `agents.md`: prompt Copilot (or your AI of choice) to turn `rules.md` into an `agents.md` file.
4.  Build the cleaning script: use `agents.md` with Copilot to generate `scripts/01_cleaning.R`.
5.  Run and verify: confirm the cleaned CSV has three columns (week, location, value) and that the epicurve is produced.

[Download slides (PPTX)](slides/day1/01_cleaning.pptx){.btn .btn-outline-primary .btn-sm}

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Preview: Activity 1, Data Cleaning and Visuals

<iframe src="https://view.officeapps.live.com/op/embed.aspx?src=https%3A%2F%2Fdma-prime.github.io%2FSISMID-2026-Human-AI-Teaming-Approach-to-ID-Modeling%2Fslides%2Fday1%2F01_cleaning.pptx" width="100%" height="600" frameborder="0">

</iframe>
:::
